comments.body: STATUS (handoff): OPEN, NOT started. DEPENDS ON the two impact-redesign children (#1 fishable-apivorous-redhead [committed 29d9fa863], #6 albite-lazy-barb [pending]) and the data-capture child (#8). Scope: permissive thresholds/flags for the new impact models (no new hard blocks at rollout); an impact_model_version sidecar tag (plan-v2/code-v2) so the calibration replay SEGMENTS old vs new formula findings (refuses to pool across versions); extend docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py to filter by impact_model_version; a diff-grounded A/B script gating any threshold-down change on beating the current model; a documented recalibration cadence. Because it references stack symbols (impact_plan/impact_code/sidecar fields), its plan-review + claim MUST be stack-grounded via REBAR_GATE_REF=<stack tip>. Implement LAST.

compiled_state.description:
# Permissive rollout + impact-model versioning + calibration tooling

## Why
The two redesigned impact models (`impact_plan`, `impact_code`) change scoring significantly.
Following the proven plan-review playbook — ship advisory/permissive, dogfood, accumulate a field
corpus, then tune blocking thresholds down only when data + an A/B justify it — this child rolls
the models out PERMISSIVELY, version-tags every review artifact so the calibration replay never
mixes old-formula and new-formula scores, and provides the offline calibration + A/B tooling that
gates any future threshold-down change on beating the current model.

## What

### A. Permissive rollout (verified, not changed)
The impact redesigns introduced NO NEW hard block. The hard-block mechanism differs by gate, so the
invariant is stated per gate — precisely what the locking test asserts, NOT a blanket claim about
every LLM criterion's posture (plan-review DOES route some structural criteria at a blocking
default_posture — those are pre-existing and untouched by the impact redesign):
- CODE-REVIEW gates blocking on an explicit `blocking_enabled: true` flag. The ONLY two criteria
  carrying it are the PRE-EXISTING `exec: DET` security detectors `secret-detection` /
  `high-critical-security` — every LLM/impact-scored criterion is `blocking_enabled: false`. So the
  impact_code redesign added no hard block. Locked by: `{c for c,v in routing if v.blocking_enabled}
  == {secret-detection, high-critical-security}`, both `exec: DET`.
- PLAN-REVIEW derives blocking from `default_posture == 'blocking'` (criteria/model.py), NOT
  `blocking_enabled` (which is absent — so a 'no blocking_enabled' check would be vacuous). It
  already has 11 pre-existing blocking-posture criteria (COH/E2/E4/F1/G1G2/G5/G6/T1/T4/T5e/T8, the
  set the #5 threshold recalibration approved). Locked by: the `default_posture == 'blocking'` set
  is EXACTLY that approved set — the impact redesign added no NEW blocking criterion; adding one
  fails the test and forces a deliberate re-approval.
So "ship permissive" is an INVARIANT to LOCK with a test (above), not a config change.

### B. Impact-model versioning (avoids corpus contamination)
Every `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecar records a top-level `impact_model_version` tag identifying the formula
that produced its scores, so the calibration replay can SEGMENT old-formula vs new-formula findings
and never pool across versions — the same cohort-tagging discipline already used for the per-finding
`cohort` carrier (plan-review `sidecar.py`), where a MISSING tag is treated as "unknown / skip".
- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py`: define a module constant `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = "plan-v2"`
  (mirroring the `EVENT_TYPE` / schema-literal constant style) and add it as a NEW top-level key in
  `build_payload`'s return dict (a scalar sibling of `schema` / `model`, stamped once per event).
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`: define `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = "code-v2"` (mirroring the
  existing `SCHEMA` constant) and add it as a NEW top-level key in `build_payload`'s return dict
  (alongside the `change_fingerprint` join key added by the data-capture child). `emit` forwards it
  automatically (the version is a module constant, not a param).
Both are additive: existing sidecar readers ignore an unknown key; the calibrator opts IN to the tag.

### C. Version-segmented calibration replay
Extend `docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` (which globs
`.tickets-tracker/*/*-REVIEW_RESULT.json` and buckets by ticket) to accept an
`--impact-model-version <v>` argument (via a NEW `argparse` in `main()`, threaded into `load()`):
when set, `load()` SKIPS any sidecar whose `data.get("impact_model_version")` != `<v>`, and a sidecar
with NO tag is treated as "unknown" and skipped (never silently pooled) — exactly the missing-tag
discipline the `cohort` carrier uses. With no `--impact-model-version`, behaviour is unchanged
(back-compat). This is the join the top-level tag in section B enables.

### D. Diff-grounded A/B harness (an ABSOLUTE, regression-detecting gate)
Add a NEW offline A/B script `docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py` that scores the checked-in
labeled/adjudicated set `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` (16 HIGH + 15 NIT,
each an `impact_code` attrs dict) with the NEW model (`impact_code`) and reports its HIGH↔NIT median
separation. The GATE is ABSOLUTE, NOT "beat the old mean": the old kernel mean `impact` CANNOT score
this fixture (the rows carry only the new consequence binaries, so it returns ~0.25 for every row),
so a "beat the baseline" gate would be trivially passable and blind to an `impact_code` regression.
Instead the script gates on the ADR-0035 separation CONTRACT — `median(HIGH) - median(NIT) > 0.30`
AND `median(NIT) < 0.30` — and EXITS NON-ZERO when it is not met, so a misconfigured `impact_code`
(e.g. collapsed tier values) that fails to separate is CAUGHT. The old mean's separation is still
PRINTED for transparency (to show it cannot discriminate) but does not gate. (Plan-review has no
checked-in labeled `.jsonl` fixture — its calibrator is driven off live sidecars via C — so the A/B
scores the code-review fixture, which is the one ADR 0035 co-calibrates against.)

### E. Documented recalibration cadence
Record the tuning cadence in an ADR under `docs/adr/`: ship permissive → accumulate version-tagged
sidecars → run the version-segmented replay (C) + the A/B (D) → propose a threshold-down ONLY when
the A/B gate passes AND the segmented corpus (this-version only) shows the separation holds at
field scale → re-run per model-version bump (each `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION` change starts a fresh cohort).

## Scope / files
- Versioning: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py`, `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`
  (the `IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION` constant + top-level payload key).
- Replay: `docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` (argparse + version filter).
- A/B: `docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py` (new).
- Cadence: `docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md` (new).
- Tests: `tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py` (payload tag + replay filter + permissive
  invariant + A/B gate).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars stamp a top-level `impact_model_version` (`plan-v2` / `code-v2`)
      from a module constant; `build_payload` includes it. Unit-tested for both payloads. Proving
      command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_model_versioning.py -v`.
- [ ] `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` accepts `--impact-model-version` and its `load()` SKIPS
      sidecars of a different version AND sidecars with NO tag (missing = unknown, never pooled);
      absent the flag, behaviour is unchanged. Unit-tested against a synthetic sidecar set.
- [ ] The permissive-rollout INVARIANT is locked by a test asserting the impact redesign added no
      NEW hard block, per gate: CODE-REVIEW's `blocking_enabled: true` set is EXACTLY
      `{secret-detection, high-critical-security}` (both `exec: DET`); PLAN-REVIEW's
      `default_posture == 'blocking'` set is EXACTLY the #5-approved 11-criterion set (so no NEW
      blocking criterion slipped in — the meaningful predicate, since plan-review has no
      `blocking_enabled` field).
- [ ] `docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py` scores `code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` with the new
      model and EXITS NON-ZERO unless it clears the ADR-0035 ABSOLUTE separation contract
      (`median(HIGH) - median(NIT) > 0.30` AND `median(NIT) < 0.30`) — a regression-detecting gate,
      NOT a trivially-passable 'beat the un-scoreable old mean' check. Unit-tested: the current
      fixture PASSES, and a degenerate constant scorer FAILS (proving the gate catches a regression).
- [ ] A recalibration-cadence ADR is recorded under `docs/adr/`. Proving command: `test -f docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md`.
compiled_state.title: Permissive rollout + calibration harness for the new impact models
